title: polypodium acrostichoides
intro: Polypodium acrostichoides, also known as the False Maidenhair Fern, is a type of plant that belongs to the Polypodiaceae family and is native to North America. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of using Polypodium acrostichoides include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the health of the digestive system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it medicinally.
